steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what the underlined sentence ("This bone is called the hyoid") does in the passage as a whole. Read what comes before and after: the sentence before names a U-shaped bone, and every sentence after uses the word "hyoid" to compare ocelots and jaguars.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The sentence explains the unique nature of the researchers' hypothesis.': The sentence only names a bone — it says nothing about why the hypothesis is unique or what makes it distinctive. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The sentence identifies the location of a bone in felines.': The sentence gives the bone a name, not a location. The passage mentions the throat earlier, but this sentence does not. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The sentence introduces a term that is used in the discussion that follows.': The sentence names the hyoid, and every subsequent sentence uses that term to contrast ocelot and jaguar anatomy. This is exactly what the sentence does. ✓
  • Choice D — 'The sentence summarizes the debate presented in the text.': There is no debate in the passage — only a hypothesis and a description. The sentence introduces a term, not a summary. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
